title: centaurea maculosa subsp. brachtii
intro: Centaurea maculosa subsp. brachtii, also known as spotted knapweed or bracht's knapweed, is a biennial plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea maculosa subsp. brachtii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
